sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The LEFT actions stopped early after 10 moves due to "noop_short_circuit" - the player can't move further left. Looking at the images, the player (green bar) is now at the far left edge. But target 2 is at column 16-19 which is still to the right of the current position. I need to check where exactly the player is now.
Let me check current positions and figure out the correct path. The player seems to have hit a wall. I need to check if there's an obstacle blocking movement.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current player and target positions
nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] >= 3:
        b = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
        max_r = max(p[0] for p in b)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"Color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=({min_r},{min_c})-({max_r},{max_c})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the issue! The player moved to the far left edge (col 0-3). But target 2 is at col 16-19, which is to the RIGHT of the current player position. The player went too far left!
Also I notice something interesting - there's a new object at (36,0)-(40,3) which is white (W) with a blue center at (37,1)-(38,2). This looks like target 3 has been relocated to the left side!
Wait, let me re-examine. Looking at the targets:
- Target 1: still at (24,44)-(27,47) with blue center - NOT destroyed
- Target 2: still at (28,16)-(31,19) with blue center - NOT destroyed  
- A new W object at (36,0)-(40,3) with blue center at (37,1)-(38,2) - this is target 3 that moved!
So the targets MOVE when the player moves! This changes everything. The targets track or move relative to the player.
The player is now at (41,0)-(43,3). Target 3 is at (36,0)-(40,3), directly above the player. I should press SPACE to destroy it, then figure out the pattern.
Let me press SPACE to destroy target 3 which is now directly above me.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Target 3 moved to (36,0)-(40,3) directly above player at (41,0)-(43,3)
# Press SPACE to destroy it
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
